name: Patient Records & Consent (AHPRA / RACGP aligned)
description: Reference policy aligned with the Medical Board of Australia's Good Medical Practice and the RACGP Standards for general practice.

data.text: Patient records & consent — AHPRA / RACGP aligned
Clinical notes are clear, accurate, and detailed enough for another doctor to continue care.
Adult patient records are retained for at least 7 years after the last contact.
Records for patients under 18 are retained until age 25, or 7 years after last contact, whichever is later.
Informed consent is documented for procedures, off-label prescribing, and any intervention with significant risks.
Prescribing of Schedule 4 and Schedule 8 medicines is documented including indication, dose, and review plan.
Discharge / handover summaries and follow-up plans are recorded.

clauses.title: Notes sufficient for handover
clauses.body: Clinical notes must be clear, accurate, and detailed enough for another doctor to continue care.

clauses.title: 7-year adult retention
clauses.body: Adult records must be retained at least 7 years after last contact.

clauses.title: Child retention to age 25
clauses.body: Records for patients under 18 must be retained until 25 or 7 years after last contact, whichever is later.

clauses.title: Informed consent documented
clauses.body: Informed consent must be documented for procedures, off-label prescribing, and any intervention with significant risks.

clauses.title: S4 / S8 prescribing documented
clauses.body: Prescribing of Schedule 4 and Schedule 8 medicines must be documented including indication, dose, and review plan.

clauses.title: Continuity of care documented
clauses.body: Discharge summaries and follow-up plans must be recorded.
